draft.action_intent: The user wants to submit the currently configured form in the playground to see its output or trigger form submission behavior.
draft.notes: This is a playground/demo environment, so 'Submit' does not send data to a real backend — it likely just displays the form data. No persistent side effects expected.

draft.action_intent: The user wants to close the samples drawer panel that lists example schema presets.
draft.notes: Closing the drawer is purely a UI toggle; the drawer can presumably be reopened. Verify whether there's a corresponding open button or if clicking the logo/header reopens it.

draft.action_intent: The user wants to navigate to the react-jsonschema-form playground home or toggle the samples/navigation drawer open.
draft.notes: This button's name is truncated and contains the app title plus theme selector text, suggesting it may be a hamburger/brand button that opens the samples drawer or acts as a home link. The exact behavior is ambiguous from the label alone — could be a drawer toggle or a brand logo link. Reviewer should verify actual click behavior.

draft.action_intent: The user wants to enter or edit the root-level form data value in the playground's rendered form preview.
draft.notes: This is a text input field labeled 'root' in the form output area. It appears to be part of the live-rendered form based on the current JSON schema. Changes are local to the playground session only.

draft.action_intent: The user wants to enter or edit a secondary form field value (labeled '_') in the playground's rendered form preview.
draft.notes: The field name '_' is unusual and likely comes from the current JSON schema definition. It sits near the 'root' input, suggesting it's another field in the same rendered form. Reviewer should confirm whether '_' is a real schema field name or a rendering artifact.

draft.action_intent: The user wants to load the 'BLANK' sample schema template into the playground editors.
draft.notes: Selecting this tab will replace the current schema, uiSchema, and formData editors with a blank template. Any unsaved edits to the current playground state would be lost. Marking semi_reversible because the user can switch to another sample but cannot automatically recover custom edits. Reviewer should verify whether there's a confirmation dialog before overwriting.
